description:
When fz-3zx switches cont keys from cycle-cut to LFP-derived,
sum's k_2 spec key widens slot 0 from `0` to `int`. Codegen
currently encodes Const::Int as RawI64 when the consumer Descr is
int (fz-ul4.27.15.1), and as Tagged otherwise. After widening,
the cont's entry-param slot expects Raw, but the producer chain
(sum's Return → cont) carries Tagged at the seam → null deref.

Goal: at every cont seam, the producer's emitted encoding must
match what the cont's spec'd slot expects under the LFP-derived
key. The producer is the eventual Return value(s) reaching the
cont through any number of intermediate frames; encoding must
agree across that whole chain.

Evidence:
  - ir_codegen::tests::sum_list_via_head_tail_recursion panics
    with null-pointer-deref at runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs:953 after
    applying the fz-3zx changes locally (see stash).
  - Every other current fixture passes — only sum hits the seam.

This must land BEFORE fz-3zx.

## Acceptance

  - Apply the fz-3zx stash; sum_list_via_head_tail_recursion
    passes without crash.
  - All five golden specs re-bless cleanly with sum's k_2 slot 0
    showing `int`.

## Investigation pointers

  - fz-ul4.27.15.1 / 15.2 — Raw-by-consumer-Descr emission.
  - fz-ul4.27.14.x — Tagged intermediate / native chain seam.
  - fz-ul4.27.16 — no-frame_ptr-in-native-fn invariant.
title: fz-3zx.0: codegen seam reconciliation for LFP-widened cont slot0 (precondition for fz-3zx)
